mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which underpins corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within 0.1%) in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 4.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a critical threshold, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, increasing the cost of capital and reducing future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand, indicating a healthy and expanding economy that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth for three consecutive months do not yield significantly positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in industrial production over a three-month period signals a weakening manufacturing sector and broader economic slowdown, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods following a 3-month cumulative decline in INDPRO exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates a fundamental expansion of the economy, which provides a strong foundation for corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term (60 days).
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where GDPC1 shows positive growth for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the year-over-year consumer price index above a 3% threshold signals persistent inflationary pressures, which can erode corporate margins and prompt aggressive central bank action, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change exceeds 3.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of consistent job creation (PAYEMS growth) and a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE decline) signifies a robust and tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth and UNRATE declines month-over-month for two consecutive months do not result in positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary or post-shock environment, a significant rebound in retail sales over a three-month period indicates renewed consumer confidence and spending, acting as a strong catalyst for corporate revenue recovery and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of periods where RSAFS 3-month cumulative percentage change exceeds 1.0% during a recognized economic recovery phase.
